Omdia estimates nearly $470 billion in total cloud commitments across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud. A significant portion of this budget can now be spent on third-party software purchases through the hyperscaler marketplaces.
In the second quarter of 2025 alone, enterprises added nearly $30 billion in new cloud commitments, underscoring the momentum behind this procurement model. What started as an opportunistic way to utilize unused cloud credits is now becoming a strategic procurement approach, where enterprises negotiate cloud deals that include marketplace budgets aligned with their cloud transformation goals.
Channel Partners Thrive in Marketplace Ecosystem
Contrary to fears that cloud marketplaces would displace traditional partners, channel partners are adapting and thriving in this new model. Omdia predicts that by 2030, partners will facilitate nearly 60% of all marketplace transactions, leveraging private offers and distributor models introduced by hyperscalers.
Partners are increasingly helping customers manage cloud commitments, purchase across multiple marketplaces, and provide end-to-end lifecycle support. With the rise of agentic AI, many partners are also building their own AI-driven platforms and services, capturing a larger share of marketplace revenues.
Foundational Software Leads, AI and Security Gain Momentum
By 2025, infrastructure software ($10.5 billion), DevOps tools ($9.1 billion), and business applications ($9.1 billion) will account for nearly 63% of marketplace spending, according to Omdia. These foundational categories continue to underpin enterprise cloud environments and modernization initiatives.
However, the AI marketplace is emerging as a major growth engine. Driven by agentic AI applications and the rise of multi-agent protocols, AI-related transactions are forecasted to reach $24.4 billion by 2030, growing at a 37 percent CAGR.
Cybersecurity is another fast-growing segment, projected to hit $31 billion by 2030 with a 31% CAGR, as enterprises invest in integrated, AI-powered security solutions to safeguard hybrid and multi-cloud ecosystems.
Hyperscalers Compete to Lead the Agentic AI Channel
“Hyperscaler marketplaces continue to see rapid momentum as a route to market for vendors across the technology industry. A small but growing number of ISVs are now reaching — and exceeding — $1 billion in annual sales through AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure marketplaces,” said Alastair Edwards, Chief Analyst at Omdia.
“Agentic AI will be one of the fastest-growing categories through marketplaces in the next five years. The hyperscalers are competing hard to win the race as a channel for agentic AI through their agent marketplaces, which are becoming a critical driver of cloud consumption,” Alastair Edwards said.
AWS Marketplace Initiatives
Private Offers and CPPO Program:
AWS introduced the Channel Partner Private Offers (CPPO) program, allowing independent software vendors (ISVs) to extend private pricing and custom contract terms through consulting partners. This enables partners to sell third-party software directly via the AWS Marketplace, helping enterprises align purchases with existing AWS cloud commitments.
AWS Marketplace for Containers and SaaS:
To support modern application development, AWS offers container-based solutions and SaaS subscriptions directly from its marketplace. Enterprises can deploy containerized apps on Amazon ECS, EKS, or Fargate with a single click.
Integration with AWS Cost Management Tools:
Enterprises can apply marketplace purchases toward their Enterprise Discount Programs (EDP), ensuring that third-party software spend contributes to meeting AWS spending commitments.
AI and ML Solutions Hub:
AWS has expanded its marketplace to include pre-trained AI and machine learning models, agentic AI tools, and data labeling services — enabling enterprises to accelerate AI adoption within existing AWS environments.
Microsoft Azure Marketplace Initiatives
Microsoft Commercial Marketplace (AppSource + Azure Marketplace):
Microsoft merged AppSource and Azure Marketplace into a unified commercial marketplace, enabling enterprise customers to discover and purchase SaaS, data, and AI solutions seamlessly integrated with Azure services.
Azure IP Co-sell Program:
Microsoft’s co-sell program incentivizes ISVs by offering joint sales motions with Microsoft sellers. This initiative helped generate billions in partner revenue by aligning Azure Marketplace solutions with Microsoft’s enterprise customer base.
Private Offers and Multi-Party Selling:
Microsoft introduced Private Offers and Multi-Party Private Offers (MPPO), allowing ISVs, distributors, and resellers to collaborate on customized deals, making enterprise procurement faster and more compliant.
Azure AI Gallery and Agentic AI Marketplace:
To capture AI-driven demand, Microsoft has added AI agents, copilots, and analytics platforms into its marketplace ecosystem. These solutions can be deployed directly within Azure’s cognitive and generative AI services.
Google Cloud Marketplace Initiatives
Integrated Billing and Spend Commitment Alignment:
Google Cloud allows enterprise customers to count marketplace purchases toward their committed cloud spend, simplifying financial governance and helping vendors gain traction among committed customers.
Google Cloud Partner Advantage and ISV Program:
Through Partner Advantage, Google enables ISVs and resellers to co-sell and co-market their offerings on the Google Cloud Marketplace, leveraging Google’s global sales and support network.
AI and Data Marketplace Expansion:
Google has built out its marketplace to feature Vertex AI models, agentic AI frameworks, and data analytics platforms, offering pre-built tools that integrate natively with BigQuery and Cloud AI.
Private Offers and Procurement APIs:
Google launched Procurement APIs that help enterprises automate approval workflows and integrate marketplace purchases into their internal procurement systems — a key feature for large organizations managing multi-cloud budgets.
Ecosystem-Wide Trends and Collaborative Initiatives
Multi-Cloud Marketplace Integrations:
Several ISVs, such as Snowflake, Palo Alto Networks, and Datadog, have built listings across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud marketplaces, ensuring enterprises can purchase through their preferred hyperscaler while using existing spend commitments.
Joint GTM Programs with ISVs:
All three hyperscalers have launched joint go-to-market programs that include marketing funds, technical enablement, and sales alignment with ISV partners to accelerate marketplace growth.
AI and Security Alliances:
Vendors like CrowdStrike, ServiceNow, and Databricks are expanding their AI-powered solutions across multiple cloud marketplaces, taking advantage of co-selling incentives and integrated deployment support from hyperscalers.
Rajani Baburajan
Baburajan Kizhakedath Baburajan Kizhakedath is the editor of InfotechLead.com. He has three decades of experience in tech media.

title_original: AI research lab NeoCognition lands $40M seed to build agents that ...
title_zh: AI研究实验室NeoCognition获4000万美元种子轮融资，用于构建智能体...
body_original:
Image Credits: NeoCognition
AI
AI research lab NeoCognition lands $40M seed to build agents that learn like humans
Marina Temkin
12:11 PM PDT · April 21, 2026
Investors are aggressively courting AI researchers to build startups that can make AI more reliable and efficient.
Yu Su , an Ohio State professor leading an AI agent lab, said he initially resisted the pressure from VCs to commercialize his work. He finally took the leap last year and spun out his work into a startup when he saw that foundational model advances could make agents truly personalized.
NeoCognition, a startup Su describes as a research lab developing self-learning AI agents, has just emerged from stealth with $40 million in seed funding. The round was co-led by Cambium Capital and Walden Catalyst Ventures, with participation from Vista Equity Partners and angels, including Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan and Databricks co-founder Ion Stoica.
“Today’s agents are generalists,” Su (pictured right) told TechCrunch. “Every time you ask them to do a task, you take a leap of faith.”
According to Su, the issue lies in a lack of consistency. Current agents, whether from Claude Code, OpenClaw, or Perplexity’s computer tools, successfully complete tasks as intended only about 50% of the time, he said.
Since agents are still so unreliable, they are not ready to be trusted, independent workers, Su told TechCrunch. NeoCognition intends to change that by developing an agent system that can self-learn to become an expert in any domain, similar to how humans learn.
Su argues that while human intelligence is broad, its real power is our ability to specialize. When we enter a new environment or profession, we can rapidly master its unique rules, relationships, and consequences.
NeoCognition is building agents to mirror this exact approach.
“For humans, our continued learning process is essentially the process of building a world model for any profession, any environment,” Su said. “We believe for agents to become experts, they need to learn autonomously to build a model of any given micro world.”
Su views this capacity for rapid specialization as the critical missing link to getting AI to work reliably on its own.
While it is possible to train agents for autonomous tasks, they must be custom-engineered for a specific vertical. NeoCognition is different because it’s building agents that are generalists capable of self-learning and specializing in any domain.
NeoCognition intends to sell its agent systems primarily to enterprises, including established SaaS companies, which can use them to build agent workers or to enhance existing product offerings.
Su highlighted that an investment from Vista Equity Partners is especially valuable for this reason. As one of the largest private equity firms in the software space, Vista can provide NeoCognition with direct access to a vast portfolio of companies looking to modernize their products with AI.
NeoCognition currently has about 15 employees, the majority of whom hold PhDs.
Topics
AI , AI agents , AI Lab , researchers , Startups
When you purchase through links in our articles, we may earn a small commission . This doesn’t affect our editorial independence.
Marina Temkin
Reporter, Venture
Marina Temkin is a venture capital and startups reporter at TechCrunch. Prior to joining TechCrunch, she wrote about VC for PitchBook and Venture Capital Journal. Earlier in her career, Marina was a financial analyst and earned a CFA charterholder designation.
You can contact or verify outreach from Marina by emailing marina.temkin@techcrunch.com or via encrypted message at +1 347-683-3909 on Signal.

This is the official github repo for pypi package ai_agent_marketplace https://pypi.org/project/ai-agent-marketplace and npm package . The repo Open Source AI Agent Marketplace and deepnlp.org website AI Agent Marketplace Store & Search Engine aims to provides a public repo, registry, subregistry (MCP Server/Skills/CLI/API registry/more) index service of more than 10K+ AI Agent from 30+ categories in the communities, such as autonomous agent, chatbots, Computer and Mobile phone use agents, robotic agents, and various industries such as business, finance, law, medical or healthcare, etc. The directory are updated to websites from both public repo (github/huggingface/producthunt/etc) as well as AI Agent services in cloud service provider (Microsoft Azure AWS, Copilot, OpenAI Agent app Store GPT Store, Google Cloud, etc).
AI Agent Marketplace Distribution By Category
We would like to help developers and end users within the lifecycle of AI agent development. From the registration, deployment, Agent router, API calling, metric/traffic tracking, and finally to the stage of moneyterization from your AI Agent. Anyone can submit their AI agents card information, code, APIs, pricing plans to the public registry, just like your submit a paper to arxiv.org and submit models to huggingface.co.
News:
OneKey Gateway is Live, Register your API to AI Agent marketplace using agtm , Distribute as as CLIs/MCPs/Skills 10x faster.
Agent & MCP Deployment Pipeline is Live: Support deployment, customized URL & Domain Verificationis: Deploy your nodejs/python agent/mcp code and get free subdomain project *.aiagenta2z.com/project_name, Deployment Workspace and Document .
Main Features
AI Agent A2Z Middleware(Deployment, Host,Payment) : Deploy your pyhon/nodejs agent/mcp code and get free subdomain Live URL *.aiagaenta2z.com/project . Get More traffic and earn credit by distribute to communities (OpenAI Apps SDK, Claude MCPs, etc. ) Doc
AI Agent Registry : Support CLIs/Skills/MCPs sub registry, you can submit your AI Agent's meta to the Official AI Agent Marketplace Registry directly on website or using agtm package. After submitting your AI Agent meta and approval, community will find your AI Agent in each categories. We also host available submission from various agent store or build in any infrastructures, such as OpenAI Apps SDK, Claude MCPs, etc.
AI Agent Search Engine and Search API : Users are able to search and explore your AI Agent through AI Agent Search Engine and you can also access the AI Agent Meta Index through Search API and MCP servers also.
OneKey Gateway : Doc AI Agent Gateway to Commercial APIs, Skills, MCPs and more using just one access key for 30+ categories APIs for most benefits.
Marketplace Domain Verification : We support subdomain verification for various platforms, such as OpenAI, WeCom, WeChat, DingTalk, etc.
AI Agent Monetization : Getting payment account for your AI Agent is not easy, we provides Stripe, Alipay, Credit card payment methods based on a unified credit charging system and and you can gain credits in your billing account from users' request. You can purchase datasets, call LLM and commercial MCPs (Google Maps, Google Search, etc) and withdraw.
Dataset for Research : We provide full snapshot of AI Agent Marketplace dataset periodically back to the community on Huggingface , so researchers can track the growth.
Agtm : Agtm CLI refers to "agent manager" cli, unifies skill management, agent registration, marketplace search, and provider CLI support, use with Claude Code/Codex/Gemini-CLI.
AI Agent Marketplace and Search Engine
AI Agent Registration
AI Agent Calls Credit Usage
Agent API Dashboard

Usage
1. AI Agent A2Z Middleware(Deployment, Host,Payment) :
Deploy your pyhon/nodejs agent/mcp code get free subdomain Live URL *.aiagaenta2z.com/project . Get More traffic and earn credit by distribute to communities (OpenAI Apps SDK, Claude MCPs, etc. ) Doc
Demo Deployed Agent and Apps
Live URL
Description
Website

A Fortune Telling Agent ChatGPT Apps Deployed Live

Perplexity API Based Deep Research Agent
Website
https://agent.deepnlp.org/agent/craftsman-agent/craftsman-agent
Craftsman-Agent generate Lego/Minecraft/Tesla Car Wrap 3D builds

2. AI Agent Registry
See detailed Doc registry.md for usage
List of Methods that you can register your AI agent. Recommended method using the Website and agtm package.
method
usage
Website
Submit and list your AI Agent meta using the website Submit official AI Agent registry Web
CLI
Command Line agtm upload --github or agtm upload --config ./agent.json
python
Install packages pip install ai-agent-marketplace
nodejs
Install packages npm install -g @aiagenta2z/agtm
CLI
Install the command line using pip or nodejs first, Get access key at Marketplace API Keys
export AI_AGENT_MARKETPLACE_ACCESS_KEY="{your_access_key}"
agtm upload --github https://github.com/AI-Hub-Admin/My-First-AI-Coding-Agent
## upload from json file or yaml file
agtm upload --config ./agent.json
agtm upload --config ./agent.yaml
3. AI Agent Search Engine and Search API :
See detailed Doc search.md for usage
Example usage
agtm search --q ' coding agent '
agtm search --id ' google-maps/google-maps '
4. OneKey Gateway
OneKey Gateway now supports use one access key to use 30+ categories commercial APIs, see for Supported APIs list .
Also, devs can ship their APIs as various agent formats 10x faster, simply register your API to OneKey Agent Gateway and distribute
as CLIs,Skills,MCPs, etc.
Ranking Top Routed Agent & MCP Ranking
npx onekey agent <unique_id> <api_id> <data_json|@file> [--timeout <ms>]
npx onekey mcp <server_name> [--name config_name] [--timeout <ms>]
npx onekey llm --provider <provider> --model <model> --messages <json|@file> [--temperature <num>] [--response-format <format>] [--options <json|@file>] [--timeout <ms>]
npx onekey llm --payload <json|@file> [--timeout <ms>]
# examples
npx onekey agent google-maps/google-maps maps_search_places '{"query":"New York City Italian Restaurants"}'
5. Traffic, API Usage Tracking, Monetization
For the deployed AI Agent and OneKey Gateway routed API, you can also check your data in API Dashboard ,
including the APIs requests and credit you consumed, and credits you earn from others people's usage of your deployed AI Agent.
Key Metrics
Some of the key metric that DeepNLP AI Agent Marketplace monitors include:
API Calling
Bing/Google search result ranking
IOS Android App Store Ranking
Github Repo Ranking
etc
You can visit DeepNLP AI Agent Marketplace and Directory to find more.
Search Ranking Performance
Suppose you want to find out some suitable AI Employees which can help you write reports or emails, schedule meeting, write codes, etc. You may use Google or Bing to search keywords like "AI Agent Employees", "Report Writing Agents", "Coding Agents", etc. And DeepNLP AI Agents Marketplace helps you monitor the AI Agents search results ranking. You can navigate to the "Email writing" tab of the AI Agent Directory and see the top reviewed AI Agents and its latested Bing Search Ranking.
And you can navigate the Email Writing AI Agents Directory and find out some top rated AI Agents in Email Writing such as
mailmeteor.com
Bing Search Rank 2.0
galaxy.ai
Bing Search Rank 3.0
6. Domain Verification
AI Agent A2Z Agent Platform provides subdomain verification. You can copy and paste the code and add a new verification records.
E.g. ChatGPT App requires users to submit the MCP urls at the same time verify they own the domains.
App
Verification URL
ChatGPT App Store
https://{username}.aiagenta2z.com/.well-known/openai-apps-challenge
MCP Official Registry
https://{username}.aiagenta2z.com/.well-known/mcp-register-challenge
WeCom (Tencent)
https://{username}.aiagenta2z.com/WW_verify_xxxxxx.txt
WeChat
https://{username}.aiagenta2z.com/MP_verify_xxxxxx.txt
DingTalk
https://{username}.aiagenta2z.com/verify_xxxxxx.txt
On the ChatGPT App Manage Page ( https://platform.openai.com/apps-manage ), you can submit your AI Agent by filling the form.
You need to prepare a MCP Server URL (e.g. https://derekzz.aiagenta2z.com/fortune-compass-agent/mcp ) and copy and paste the
verification code under the file path ( https://derekzz.aiagenta2z.com/.well-known/openai-apps-challenge ).
You can go to the Domain Verification tab of AI Agent A2Z Deployment ( https://deepnlp.org/workspace/deploy ) platform.
Fill the form of openai-apps-challenge with the code on the platform. Then click Verify Domain to add a record.
Please wait a while for the record to work. If you want to change the content, just add a new record and the content will be overridden.
Contributing
You are welcome to contribute to the AGENT.md to include links and introduction to your AI Agent.
Please follow the contributing guideline CONTRIBUTING.md .

title_original: Agent Distribution Wars 2026: GPT Store vs Claude Marketplace vs GitHub MCP | AgentMarketCap
body_original:
Three platforms. Three incompatible theories of how AI agents should be discovered, distributed, and monetized. Whoever wins this fight will effectively become the App Store of the agentic era — a position worth far more than the hundreds of millions of dollars currently flowing through these nascent marketplaces.
As of Q1 2026, the battle lines are clear: OpenAI's GPT Store is the incumbent consumer play, Anthropic's Claude Marketplace is the aggressive enterprise challenger with a zero-commission model, and GitHub's MCP Registry is the developer-native dark horse betting that open standards beat walled gardens every time. The agentic AI market is already projected to hit $10.8 billion in 2026 and expand to nearly $200 billion by 2034 — but the critical question isn't who builds the best agents. It's who owns the pipe through which those agents reach buyers.
The Incumbent: OpenAI's GPT Store and the Creator Economy Bet
OpenAI launched its GPT Store in January 2024 with a bold promise: custom AI agents would follow the trajectory of iPhone apps, minting a new class of AI-native entrepreneurs. By early 2026, the store hosts over three million custom GPTs — more applications than most enterprise software catalogues have seen in their entire lifetimes.
The reality of creator economics, however, has been more sobering than the headline number suggests. OpenAI's revenue-sharing program pays approximately $0.03 per conversation, meaning a creator needs more than 33,000 quality interactions per month to clear $1,000 in earnings. The eligibility bar is steep: US-based account, public GPT, and a sustained 25+ conversations per week. Most builders never qualify. Those who do typically earn $100–500 per month, with only a tiny fraction reaching the mid-four-figures range.
What salvaged the GPT Store thesis was a product shift, not a monetization fix. The release of GPT-5.2 in December 2025 fundamentally transformed custom GPTs from "enhanced chatbots" into what developers are now calling "agentic mini-apps" — systems that can call external tools, maintain state across sessions, and execute multi-step workflows autonomously. Savvy builders responded by abandoning viral consumer GPTs entirely and pivoting to B2B consulting tools and enterprise internal deployments, where value is easier to demonstrate and willingness to pay is measurably higher.
This pivot has meaningful implications for the Store's distribution model. OpenAI built its marketplace assuming a consumer discovery metaphor — users browse, try, subscribe. What's emerging instead is closer to an enterprise sales motion dressed in a storefront UI. The GPT Store's browse-and-discover interface was optimized for the wrong buyer persona, and OpenAI hasn't yet rebuilt it for the B2B reality that its most valuable creators have already moved toward.
The Challenger: Anthropic's Zero-Commission Gambit
Anthropic's entry into marketplace distribution was, by the standards of the industry, unusually transparent about its strategic intent. In March 2026, the company launched the Claude Marketplace — and announced it would take zero commission on transactions.
To understand why this is remarkable, consider what "zero cut" means against the competitive backdrop. AWS Marketplace and Azure Marketplace typically extract between 3% and 30% of each transaction, depending on product category. Apple takes 15–30% from App Store developers. Anthropic is explicitly forgoing this revenue stream at launch, a decision that signals one thing loudly: deepening enterprise lock-in is currently worth more to Anthropic than transaction revenue.
The launch catalog was deliberately curated rather than open. Six partners anchored the initial marketplace: GitLab, Harvey AI, Lovable, Replit, Rogo, and Snowflake. Each represents a different enterprise vertical — developer tooling, legal AI, low-code creation, collaborative coding, financial intelligence, and data cloud. The selection isn't random; it's a statement about which enterprise software categories Anthropic believes agents will disrupt first.
The procurement mechanic is cleverly engineered. Enterprise customers with committed annual API spending can apply a portion of that commitment toward partner tools — and Anthropic handles all invoicing. This makes Anthropic the central clearinghouse for enterprise AI procurement, not just an infrastructure provider. Partners gain immediate access to an installed base of enterprise customers who have already completed security reviews, signed contracts, and allocated budget. Anthropic gains stickiness that goes well beyond API calls.
Simultaneously, Anthropic's Claude Cowork platform shipped 13 new MCP connectors in February 2026, covering Google Workspace (Drive, Calendar, Gmail), DocuSign, Apollo, Clay, Outreach, SimilarWeb, MSCI, LegalZoom, FactSet, WordPress, and Harvey. Enterprises can now build private internal marketplaces — curated catalogs of specialized agents distributed across their organizations without exposing those agents to the public marketplace. This two-tier approach (public marketplace for ecosystem, private marketplace for enterprise customization) is structurally similar to Salesforce AppExchange's public/private distribution model.
The Dark Horse: GitHub's MCP Registry and the Open Standard Play
While OpenAI and Anthropic fight to own proprietary distribution channels, GitHub is betting on a different theory entirely: that the agent layer will be governed by an open standard the way the web was governed by HTTP, and that the platform controlling the canonical registry for that standard will own discovery by default.
The Model Context Protocol (MCP) — originally developed by Anthropic but donated to the Linux Foundation and now adopted by Anthropic, OpenAI, Microsoft, and Google — has become what developers are calling "the USB-C for AI." It provides a standardized interface for agents to connect to external tools, data sources, and services without bespoke integrations.
GitHub launched its MCP Registry in September 2025 as the central discovery hub for MCP servers. By early 2026, the registry hosts 923+ MCP servers covering everything from Playwright-based browser automation to Notion knowledge access to GitHub's own MCP server with over a hundred tools. This isn't a curated enterprise catalog — it's a developer-native ecosystem index, more similar to npm or PyPI than to an app store.
The GitHub angle is reinforced by raw repository growth. GitHub's Octoverse 2025 report revealed 4.3 million AI-related repositories on the platform — a 178% year-over-year increase in LLM-focused projects. GitHub Copilot Agent Mode, which rolled out to all VS Code users in April 2025 with full MCP support, gives every developer a direct pathway from MCP discovery to MCP deployment without leaving their IDE.
The limitation of this approach is commercialization. GitHub's MCP Registry, like npm, is a discovery mechanism rather than a transaction layer. There's no built-in monetization, no procurement integration, and no enterprise contract consolidation. Developers love it. CFOs have never heard of it.
Three Strategies, Three Different Markets
The divergence in approach maps directly onto the three distinct buyer personas these platforms are optimizing for:
Platform Primary Buyer Commission Model Distribution Logic
OpenAI GPT Store Consumers & SMBs $0.03/conversation Browse-and-discover
Anthropic Claude Marketplace Enterprise procurement teams 0% (no cut) Spend consolidation
GitHub MCP Registry Individual developers Free (no monetization) Open standard index
None of these is wrong in isolation. The problem is that the market hasn't decided which buyer persona matters most at the current stage of agentic AI adoption — and all three platforms are making different bets on that answer.
Salesforce's trajectory with Agentforce offers a data point worth noting: it hit $540 million in annual recurring revenue and 18,500 enterprise customers by early 2026 through pure enterprise sales, with no consumer marketplace at all. If the enterprise bet is correct, Anthropic's zero-commission model looks prescient. If consumer adoption drives enterprise pull-through (as happened with Slack, Zoom, and Dropbox), OpenAI's creator ecosystem looks like the right foundation. If developers standardize on MCP before the distribution wars are decided, GitHub's registry position becomes defensible in ways that proprietary marketplaces cannot easily replicate.
The Consolidation Question
A single analogy structures most predictions about this market: the early smartphone ecosystem had dozens of app stores before network effects collapsed the market to two dominant platforms. Will the same happen with agent distribution?
The conditions are partially analogous. Discovery is a genuine problem — with 10,000+ agent applications and growing, buyers increasingly need curated, searchable, trustworthy catalogues. Trust and security review are enterprise-critical, which creates a natural moat for platforms that have already completed procurement compliance work. And the distribution layer is sticky: agents that are already deployed inside enterprise workflows, already credentialed against enterprise data sources, and already invoiced through a consolidated procurement channel, are very hard to move.
But MCP complicates the smartphone analogy in a meaningful way. The App Store's moat depended on closed APIs and exclusive distribution. MCP, by design, is open and interoperable. An agent built for the Claude Marketplace can, in principle, be served through the GitHub MCP Registry with minimal modification. The distribution wars are happening in a world where the underlying connector protocol is already standardized — which may limit how durable any single platform's marketplace moat can actually be.
The fragmentation we're witnessing in early 2026 may be less "winner-take-all consolidation incoming" and more "platform differentiation by vertical" — where OpenAI owns consumer and SMB discovery, Anthropic owns enterprise procurement consolidation, and GitHub owns developer-native tooling distribution. All three could sustain meaningful positions without any one of them winning outright.
What This Means for Agent Builders
For teams building AI agents today, the distribution question has immediate strategic implications:
If you're targeting enterprise procurement cycles , the Claude Marketplace's zero-commission model and spend consolidation mechanics represent a structurally superior go-to-market path right now. Being in front of buyers who have already committed budget and completed security reviews is more valuable than organic discovery traffic.
If you're targeting developer adoption , MCP compliance and a GitHub MCP Registry listing is effectively table stakes. The 923+ servers already indexed represent a competitive set, not a validation of success — but absence from the registry is a meaningful trust signal deficiency.
If you're targeting consumer or SMB buyers , the GPT Store's 910 million weekly active users on ChatGPT remain an unmatched distribution surface, despite the thin creator economics. The challenge is the B2B pivot the most successful builders have already made: the consumer UI doesn't serve B2B discovery well, and OpenAI hasn't fixed it yet.
The uncomfortable reality for most agent builders is that distribution strategy can't be deferred until after product-market fit. The three platforms have different technical requirements, different compliance expectations, and different buyer relationships. Building for one often means not being optimized for another.
The $500M Question
Current estimates put the agent marketplace economy at roughly $500 million in 2026 — a number that will look trivially small against the $199 billion projected agentic AI market by 2034. The platform that wins the distribution layer won't just earn marketplace fees; it will set the pricing norms, security standards, compliance requirements, and integration expectations for an entire category of software.
OpenAI has the audience. Anthropic has the enterprise relationships and the boldest commercial positioning. GitHub has the developer trust and the open standard. The distribution wars of 2026 are still early enough that none of these advantages is decisive — and late enough that the patterns being established now will be very hard to reverse.
Explore the full AI agent rankings at AgentMarketCap to compare capabilities, benchmarks, and market positions across 500+ agents in the ecosystem.
Back to all posts
AgentMarketCap — Track and discover AI agents. Scores are composite benchmarks and may not reflect real-world performance.

title_original: October '25 enterprise roundup - GitHub
title_zh: 2025年10月企业动态汇总 - GitHub
body_original:
This Roundup is a must-read for any software developer or engineering leader navigating the rapidly evolving landscape of AI-powered development, security, and platform governance. We have curated the most impactful updates, best practices, and strategic insights from GitHub’s own engineering playbook, blog posts, changelogs, whitepapers and other resources—each link is handpicked to help you and your teams ship faster, reduce toil, and unlock new levels of developer creativity.
We continue to look for additional resources that will enable you to get the most out of GitHub and we have uncovered a new resource this month, the GitHub at Work Podcast . This is a great resource if you like to use your commute time, run/walk time, etc. as an opportunity to continually learn about the latest and greatest from GitHub.
We don't expect every person to read every word of this post. Skim through the topics that apply to how you and your teams use GitHub and dig into links that are the most relevant to you. Since some readers may skip over entire sections, you may see the same link appear in multiple sections such as a link that applies to both Code Security and CI/CD. Pass this Enterprise Roundup along to your colleagues or pass along specific links that will be beneficial to others.
Analyst Reports
📚 GitHub recognized as a Leader in the Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for AI Code Assistants - GitHub Copilot has been named a Leader in Gartner’s 2025 Magic Quadrant for AI Code Assistants—placing highest in Ability to Execute and furthest in Completeness of Vision —for the second year in a row. Gartner predicts that by 2028, 90% of enterprise software engineers will be using AI code assistants, and GitHub’s scale (20M+ users across 77K enterprises), agent‑based workflows, hybrid deployment flexibility, robust IDE and DevSecOps integrations, and deep enterprise-grade maturity position it as the tool enterprises need now. Understanding how GitHub continues to innovate—such as through Copilot Coding Agent, App Modernization, and enterprise-scale governance—will help software leaders evaluate whether Copilot can deliver productivity gains, code quality improvements, and scalable AI adoption across their teams.
Events
While GitHub hosts our own marquee events like Universe and Galaxy each year, you will also find GitHub participating in other industry events. Here is the latest news about upcoming events.
📅 GitHub Universe
October 28–29 at the historic Fort Mason in San Francisco. It’s our biggest event of the year—bringing together enterprise leaders and engineering experts shaping the future with AI. We have a lineup of over 100 sessions, demos, and expert panels focused on AI-powered development, enterprise-grade automation, and security best practices, featuring insights from leaders at Red Hat, GM, CVS, and more. This event also offers exclusive one-on-one mentoring and immersive hands-on labs—delivering the strategic know-how and technical depth your team needs to innovate, scale securely, and confidently drive business impact. New this year: Each general admission pass includes a GitHub certification exam, so your teams will have even more skills to bring back to your organization. Check out this blog post which provides a sampling of key sessions: 📢 Your guide to GitHub Universe 2025
📅 Microsoft Ignite
Get the edge you need to drive impact in the era of AI. Join us to bolster your knowledge, build connections, and explore emerging technologies. San Francisco, Moscone Center November 18–21, 2025 and Online November 18–20, 2025. You’ll learn about the latest tech trends and innovations that can help your organization gain a competitive edge and drive impact in the era of AI. Plus, you’ll get hands-on experience with cutting-edge AI, security, and IT solutions to bolster your knowledge and expand your expertise. It’s also a chance to connect with GitHub and Microsoft experts, partners, executives, and customers, allowing you to grow our network. Check out the more than 30 GitHub related sessions .
📅 Check out the complete upcoming conference schedule and upcoming webinar schedule .
GitHub Spark (Preview)
With GitHub Spark , you can describe what you want in natural language and get a fullstack web app with data storage, AI features, and GitHub authentication built in. You can iterate using prompts, visual tools, or code, and then deploy with a click to a fully managed runtime. Spark is seamlessly integrated with GitHub so you can develop your spark via a synced GitHub codespace with Copilot for advanced editing. You can also create a repository for team collaboration, and leverage GitHub's ecosystem of tools and integrations. Consider using GitHub Spark to quickly create a full stack prototype and then hand the repo over to pro devs to turn it into a production ready solution.
🚢 GitHub Spark in public preview for Copilot Enterprise subscribers
Accelerate your team’s innovation cycle by seamlessly turning ideas into live prototypes—and even production-ready apps—directly within GitHub, using AI-powered Spark with no setup and one-click deployment for Copilot Enterprise subscribers.
🚢 Share read-only Sparks with controlled data access
You can now securely share read‑only Spark applications—letting enterprise teams expose interactive insights without risking data integrity—while also benefiting from improved JSON mode, editor sync, and loading reliability.
For more information, refer to the documentation:
📄 About GitHub Spark
📄 Managing GitHub Spark in your enterprise
📄 Write effective prompts and provide useful context for Spark
AI & ML - GitHub Copilot
Recent advancements and feature updates for GitHub Copilot, with a particular focus on the coding agent and agent mode. Key updates include new ways to assign work to the GitHub Copilot coding agent e.g. from the CLI, Azure Boards and Microsoft Teams as well as GitHub Copilot app modernization for Java and .NET, a new MCP server registry, aggregate context for GitHub Copilot with Copilot Spaces and more!
NOTE: Key capabilities that are still in Preview as of 2025-10-04 are: GitHub Spark, Upgrade assistant for Java in VS Code, Code feedback in VS Code. To quickly see which GitHub Copilot capabilities are in Preview, go to GitHub Copilot · Your AI pair programmer , Click on "For Business" and scroll down to see a complete list of features. This list highlights which features are Preview.
GitHub Copilot coding agent and Agent Mode
🚢 Copilot coding agent is now generally available!
Enhance your development workflow instantly with the GitHub Copilot coding agent—now GA—which autonomously drafts PRs, addresses bugs, technical debt, feature work, tests, and docs and integrates seamlessly across GitHub and your IDE of choice, scalable for enterprise-level teams under admin-controlled policy.
📢 & 📺 GitHub Copilot coding agent 101: Getting started with agentic workflows on GitHub
(9:06) - GitHub’s new Copilot coding agent acts like an asynchronous AI teammate—spin up a task from an issue, let it autonomously create a branch, implement code, run tests via GitHub Actions, and open a pull request for your review. It’s designed to offload time-consuming, low-to-medium complexity work—like refactoring, bug fixes, and improving test coverage—so senior developers and team leads can focus on high-impact design and architecture, all within an enterprise-grade, secure, and auditable workflow.
🚢 Assign Azure Boards work items to Copilot coding agent in public preview
Connects Azure Boards tasks to Copilot for automated progress and completion tracking.
🚢 Work with Copilot coding agent in Microsoft Teams
Integrates AI coding assistance directly into Teams for seamless collaboration between developers and stakeholders.
🚢 Kick off and track Copilot coding agent sessions from the GitHub CLI
Enables developers to launch and monitor AI‑powered coding sessions directly from the command line.
🚢 Copilot coding agent remembers context within the same pull request
Keeps AI suggestions consistent and relevant by retaining PR‑specific context across multiple interactions.
🎧 GitHub Copilot's September Updates and Agentic Future | GitHub at Work Podcast
This episode includes a deep dive into GitHub Universe, agentic development, and the MCP Server's open-sourcing and security.
📢 Spec-driven development with AI: Get started with a new open source toolkit
If you're building enterprise-grade software with AI coding agents like GitHub Copilot, this post introduces a game-changing approach: spec-driven development. GitHub’s new open source toolkit, Spec Kit, transforms vague prompts into structured, executable specs—making AI workflows more reliable, scalable, and aligned with real-world constraints. Learn how to shift from “vibe-coding” to intentional, high-quality development that integrates seamlessly with your architecture, compliance, and design systems.
📢 Spec-driven development: Using Markdown as a programming language when building with AI
Discover how spec-driven development using Markdown as a programming language—paired with AI coding agents like GitHub Copilot—can streamline your workflow, reduce context loss, and accelerate iteration cycles. This post introduces a practical, open-source approach that keeps documentation and implementation in sync, enabling teams to build more maintainable and scalable software with less friction.
🚢 GitHub Copilot app modernization is now generally available for Java and .NET
With the ability to assess legacy Java and .NET applications, apply automated code transformations, patch builds, update dependencies, and containerize services in days instead of months, GitHub Copilot’s new app modernization features empower enterprise teams to accelerate migrations and upgrades with practical, end‑to‑end assistance.
📢 A step-by-step guide to modernizing Java projects with GitHub Copilot agent mode
Harness GitHub Copilot’s new Agent Mode to automatically scan, upgrade, and harden your legacy Java codebase—from deprecated APIs and insecure dependencies to full cloud-readiness with Azure migration—
without manual scripting or guesswork . By transforming Copilot into an AI-powered junior engineer , this guide delivers a step-by-step, feedback-driven modernization workflow that slashes manual effort, eliminates build errors, and fast-tracks secure, cloud-native deployments.
🚢 Pick the repository and base branch when assigning issues to Copilot - Adds precision to AI‑driven issue assignment by letting you target specific repos and branches.
Getting the Most from GitHub Copilot across the Enterprise
📢 GitHub Copilot gets smarter at finding your code: Inside our new embedding model
GitHub Copilot’s new embedding model dramatically improves code search in VS Code—delivering faster, more accurate, and memory-efficient results. With a 37.6% boost in retrieval quality and over 100% improvement in code acceptance for Java and C# developers, this update directly enhances productivity and reliability in large-scale, real-world development workflows. If your team relies on Copilot, understanding this upgrade is key to unlocking smarter, context-aware coding assistance.
📚 Playbook series: Why you need a DRI for your AI program
This post reveals why appointing a Directly Responsible Individual (DRI) —a dedicated leader, strategist, coach, and data storyteller—is the critical linchpin for transforming an AI vision into tangible business outcomes. Learn how GitHub sets this role up for success, and why without a DRI your AI investments risk staying siloed, underutilized, or stuck in “strategy” mode rather than delivering real results.
📚 Playbook series: Fostering AI learning opportunities
Looking to transform your organization's AI tools into real-world impact? This post reveals why access alone isn’t enough—and outlines a proven blueprint for scaling AI fluency: from curated learning hubs and structured paths to real-world use cases and live “office hours” sessions—empowering every developer and leader to go from curiosity to confident, productive AI usage.
📢 5 tips for writing better custom instructions for Copilot
If you're using GitHub Copilot in your enterprise projects, writing effective custom instructions is no longer optional—it’s essential. This guide shows how a well-crafted copilot-instructions.md file can dramatically improve Copilot’s code suggestions by giving it the context your team already knows but the AI doesn’t. Learn how to turn Copilot into a productive teammate by sharing your tech stack, coding standards, and project structure the right way.
📄 Customization library - GitHub Docs
We've published a curated collection of examples you can copy, adjust, and use to enhance your experience with Copilot. This library is designed to inspire and educate people on the options available to customize Copilot responses. We've included examples of custom instructions (widely supported) and prompt files (supported in VS Code only). The examples cover scenarios such as debugging, onboarding, and accessibility.
🙋‍♂️ Mastering-GitHub-Copilot-for-Paired-Programming: A multi-module course teaching everything you need to know about using GitHub Copilot as an AI Peer Programming resource
Mastering Copilot isn’t optional—it’s the new literacy for modern devs. We've recently updated and launched a free, hands-on course that shows you how to pair program with Copilot like a pro—writing smarter code, solving real problems, and unlocking AI workflows that actually scale.
🚢 Manage Copilot and users via Enterprise Teams in public preview
Centralizes AI user and license management for large organizations.
🚢 Premium requests analytics page is now generally available
See how your organization’s premium request usage translates into real costs and gain granular insights—down to users, models, and cost centers—via a new Copilot analytics dashboard and API endpoint available for enterprise and billing teams.
GitHub Copilot and Model Context Protocol (MCP) Servers
📢 Meet the GitHub MCP Registry: The fastest way to discover MCP Servers
Discover how the new GitHub MCP Registry dramatically simplifies and secures the discovery of MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers—centralizing what was previously scattered across repos, threads, and multiple registries into a curated, one-click marketplace built right into VS Code and Copilot. For more information refer to 📄 About Model Context Protocol (MCP) & 📄 Extending GitHub Copilot Chat with the Model Context Protocol (MCP) .
📺 A deep dive into the GitHub MCP registry
(9:13) - See how the GitHub MCP registry and its open source governance model are transforming the way developers discover, vet, and publish high-quality tools—making it easier than ever to drive innovation securely at scale within enterprise environments. Learn why self-publication, robust API integration, and curated allowlists are game-changers for organizations seeking to accelerate developer productivity and confidently adopt new technologies.
🚢 Internal MCP registry and allowlist controls for VS Code Insiders
Enterprise teams can now securely configure and enforce trusted MCP server access in VS Code Insiders
—enabling early testing ahead of the full Copilot rollout.
🚢 Remote GitHub MCP Server is now generally available
This enables enterprise developers to securely connect AI agents to live GitHub data—like issues, pull requests, and code files—using OAuth, one-click install, and automatic updates and without local setup.
📢 & 📺 How to debug a web app with Playwright MCP and GitHub Copilot
Discover how GitHub Copilot, empowered by the Playwright MCP server, can autonomously reproduce, diagnose, and validate web app bugs—dramatically reducing manual debugging effort. If your team struggles with incomplete test coverage or tedious repro steps, this guide shows how to turn Copilot into a hands-on debugging assistant that sees and interacts with your app like a real user.
📢 & 📺 Building smarter interactions with MCP elicitation: From clunky tool calls to seamless user experiences
Traditional AI tool integrations often rely on rigid assumptions and hard-coded defaults, leading to clunky user experiences. This post introduces MCP elicitation , a new capability that enables AI agents like GitHub Copilot to dynamically ask for missing information—transforming interactions from brittle tool calls into intuitive, context-aware workflows.
Copilot Spaces
🚢 Copilot Spaces is now generally available - Copilot Spaces let you organize the context that Copilot uses to answer your questions. Spaces can include repositories, code, pull requests, issues, free-text content like transcripts or notes, images, and file uploads. You can ask Copilot questions grounded in that context, or share the space with your team to support collaboration and knowledge sharing. For more information refer to:
📄 About GitHub Copilot Spaces
📄 Creating GitHub Copilot Spaces
📄 Using GitHub Copilot Spaces
GitHub Copilot CLI
🚢 GitHub Copilot CLI is now in public preview - Brings AI‑driven coding assistance to terminal workflows for faster, smarter development. For more information refer to the documentation:
📄 About GitHub Copilot CLI
📄 Using GitHub Copilot CLI
📺 How to use the GitHub Copilot CLI to onboard, debug, and contribute
(6:18) - Discover how GitHub Copilot CLI transforms developer productivity by seamlessly integrating AI-powered collaboration directly into your terminal, eliminating the hassle of API keys and streamlining everything from onboarding to issue resolution. Learn why this tool is becoming the main AI collaborator for leading teams, empowering you to automate common tasks, accelerate codebase understanding, and adopt best practices.
🚢 GitHub Copilot CLI: Enhanced model selection, image support, and streamlined UI
GitHub Copilot CLI now delivers enterprise-grade flexibility and efficiency with preview access to Anthropic’s adva
body_clean:
This Roundup is a must-read for any software developer or engineering leader navigating the rapidly evolving landscape of AI-powered development, security, and platform governance. We have curated the most impactful updates, best practices, and strategic insights from GitHub’s own engineering playbook, blog posts, changelogs, whitepapers and other resources—each link is handpicked to help you and your teams ship faster, reduce toil, and unlock new levels of developer creativity.
We continue to look for additional resources that will enable you to get the most out of GitHub and we have uncovered a new resource this month, the GitHub at Work Podcast . This is a great resource if you like to use your commute time, run/walk time, etc. as an opportunity to continually learn about the latest and greatest from GitHub.
We don't expect every person to read every word of this post. Skim through the topics that apply to how you and your teams use GitHub and dig into links that are the most relevant to you. Since some readers may skip over entire sections, you may see the same link appear in multiple sections such as a link that applies to both Code Security and CI/CD. Pass this Enterprise Roundup along to your colleagues or pass along specific links that will be beneficial to others.
Analyst Reports
📚 GitHub recognized as a Leader in the Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for AI Code Assistants - GitHub Copilot has been named a Leader in Gartner’s 2025 Magic Quadrant for AI Code Assistants—placing highest in Ability to Execute and furthest in Completeness of Vision —for the second year in a row. Gartner predicts that by 2028, 90% of enterprise software engineers will be using AI code assistants, and GitHub’s scale (20M+ users across 77K enterprises), agent‑based workflows, hybrid deployment flexibility, robust IDE and DevSecOps integrations, and deep enterprise-grade maturity position it as the tool enterprises need now. Understanding how GitHub continues to innovate—such as through Copilot Coding Agent, App Modernization, and enterprise-scale governance—will help software leaders evaluate whether Copilot can deliver productivity gains, code quality improvements, and scalable AI adoption across their teams.
Events
While GitHub hosts our own marquee events like Universe and Galaxy each year, you will also find GitHub participating in other industry events. Here is the latest news about upcoming events.
📅 GitHub Universe
October 28–29 at the historic Fort Mason in San Francisco. It’s our biggest event of the year—bringing together enterprise leaders and engineering experts shaping the future with AI. We have a lineup of over 100 sessions, demos, and expert panels focused on AI-powered development, enterprise-grade automation, and security best practices, featuring insights from leaders at Red Hat, GM, CVS, and more. This event also offers exclusive one-on-one mentoring and immersive hands-on labs—delivering the strategic know-how and technical depth your team needs to innovate, scale securely, and confidently drive business impact. New this year: Each general admission pass includes a GitHub certification exam, so your teams will have even more skills to bring back to your organization. Check out this blog post which provides a sampling of key sessions: 📢 Your guide to GitHub Universe 2025
📅 Microsoft Ignite
Get the edge you need to drive impact in the era of AI. Join us to bolster your knowledge, build connections, and explore emerging technologies. San Francisco, Moscone Center November 18–21, 2025 and Online November 18–20, 2025. You’ll learn about the latest tech trends and innovations that can help your organization gain a competitive edge and drive impact in the era of AI. Plus, you’ll get hands-on experience with cutting-edge AI, security, and IT solutions to bolster your knowledge and expand your expertise. It’s also a chance to connect with GitHub and Microsoft experts, partners, executives, and customers, allowing you to grow our network. Check out the more than 30 GitHub related sessions .
📅 Check out the complete upcoming conference schedule and upcoming webinar schedule .
GitHub Spark (Preview)
With GitHub Spark , you can describe what you want in natural language and get a fullstack web app with data storage, AI features, and GitHub authentication built in. You can iterate using prompts, visual tools, or code, and then deploy with a click to a fully managed runtime. Spark is seamlessly integrated with GitHub so you can develop your spark via a synced GitHub codespace with Copilot for advanced editing. You can also create a repository for team collaboration, and leverage GitHub's ecosystem of tools and integrations. Consider using GitHub Spark to quickly create a full stack prototype and then hand the repo over to pro devs to turn it into a production ready solution.

title_original: SambaNova Launches Its AI Platform in AWS Marketplace
title_zh: SambaNova 在 AWS 市场推出其 AI 平台
body_original:
SambaNova Launches Its AI Platform in AWS Marketplace
Share
Organizations can now seamlessly purchase and deploy SambaNova’s fast inference services alongside their existing infrastructure in AWS.
PALO ALTO, Calif.--( BUSINESS WIRE )-- SambaNova , the AI inference company delivering fast, efficient AI chips and high-performance models, today announced that its AI platform is now available in AWS Marketplace , a digital catalog that helps you find, buy, deploy, and manage software, data products, and professional services from thousands of vendors. This availability allows organizations to seamlessly purchase and deploy SambaNova’s fast inference services alongside their existing infrastructure in AWS .
By leveraging existing AWS relationships, organizations can now begin using SambaNova’s advanced inference solutions with a few simple clicks — accelerating time to value while maintaining trusted billing and infrastructure practices.
Share
This new availability marks a significant milestone in SambaNova’s mission to make private, production-grade AI more accessible to enterprises, removing traditional barriers like vendor onboarding and procurement delays. By leveraging existing AWS relationships, organizations can now begin using SambaNova’s advanced inference solutions with a few simple clicks — accelerating time to value while maintaining trusted billing and infrastructure practices.
“Enterprises face significant pressure to move rapidly from AI experimentation to full-scale production, yet procurement and integration challenges often stand in the way,” said Rodrigo Liang, CEO and co-founder of SambaNova. “By offering SambaNova’s platform in AWS Marketplace, we remove those obstacles, enabling organizations to access our industry leading inference solutions instantly, using the procurement processes and a cloud environment they already trust.”
Accelerating Access to High-Performance Inference
SambaNova’s listing in AWS Marketplace gives customers the ability to:
Procure through existing AWS billing arrangements — no new vendor setup required.
Leverage SambaNova’s inference performance — fast and efficiently, running open source models like Llama 4 Maverick and DeepSeek R1 671B.
Engage securely via private connectivity — possible through AWS PrivateLink for low-latency, secure integration between AWS workloads and SambaNova Cloud.
“With the SambaNova platform running in AWS Marketplace, organizations gain access to secure, high-speed inference from the largest open-source models. Solutions like this will help businesses move from experimentation to full production with AI,” said Michele Rosen, Research Manager, Open GenAI, LLMs, and the Evolving Open Source, IDC.
This tight integration enables customers to deploy high-performance, multi-tenant inference solutions without the need to purchase or manage custom hardware — expanding SambaNova’s reach into enterprise environments where time-to-value and IT friction have historically limited adoption.
Making High-Performance Inference More Accessible
With this listing in AWS Marketplace, SambaNova is meeting enterprise customers where they already are — within their trusted cloud environments and procurement frameworks. By removing onboarding friction and offering seamless integration, SambaNova makes it easier than ever for organizations to evaluate, deploy, and scale high-performance inference solutions.
“This makes it dramatically easier for customers to start using SambaNova — no new contracts, no long onboarding — just click and go,” said Liang.
Availability
SambaNova’s inference platform is available immediately in AWS Marketplace. Enterprise customers can visit the SambaNova listing in AWS Marketplace to get started.
About SambaNova
Customers turn to SambaNova to quickly deploy state-of-the-art generative AI capabilities within the enterprise. Our purpose-built enterprise-scale AI platform is the technology backbone for the next generation of AI computing.
Headquartered in Palo Alto, California, SambaNova Systems was founded in 2017 by industry luminaries, and hardware and software design experts from Sun/Oracle and Stanford University. Investors include SoftBank Vision Fund 2, funds and accounts managed by BlackRock, Intel Capital, GV, Walden International, Temasek, GIC, Redline Capital, Atlantic Bridge Ventures, Celesta, and several others. Visit us at sambanova.ai or contact us at info@sambanova.ai . Follow SambaNova Systems on LinkedIn and on X . To learn more, visit www.sambanova.ai
Contacts
Virginia Jamieson, Head of External Communications, SambaNova
virginia.jamieson@sambanova.ai

SambaNova Launches Its AI Platform in AWS Marketplace
Share
Organizations can now seamlessly purchase and deploy SambaNova’s fast inference services alongside their existing infrastructure in AWS.
PALO ALTO, Calif.--( BUSINESS WIRE )-- SambaNova , the AI inference company delivering fast, efficient AI chips and high-performance models, today announced that its AI platform is now available in AWS Marketplace , a digital catalog that helps you find, buy, deploy, and manage software, data products, and professional services from thousands of vendors. This availability allows organizations to seamlessly purchase and deploy SambaNova’s fast inference services alongside their existing infrastructure in AWS .
By leveraging existing AWS relationships, organizations can now begin using SambaNova’s advanced inference solutions with a few simple clicks — accelerating time to value while maintaining trusted billing and infrastructure practices.
Share
This new availability marks a significant milestone in SambaNova’s mission to make private, production-grade AI more accessible to enterprises, removing traditional barriers like vendor onboarding and procurement delays. By leveraging existing AWS relationships, organizations can now begin using SambaNova’s advanced inference solutions with a few simple clicks — accelerating time to value while maintaining trusted billing and infrastructure practices.
“Enterprises face significant pressure to move rapidly from AI experimentation to full-scale production, yet procurement and integration challenges often stand in the way,” said Rodrigo Liang, CEO and co-founder of SambaNova. “By offering SambaNova’s platform in AWS Marketplace, we remove those obstacles, enabling organizations to access our industry leading inference solutions instantly, using the procurement processes and a cloud environment they already trust.”
Accelerating Access to High-Performance Inference
SambaNova’s listing in AWS Marketplace gives customers the ability to:
Procure through existing AWS billing arrangements — no new vendor setup required.
Leverage SambaNova’s inference performance — fast and efficiently, running open source models like Llama 4 Maverick and DeepSeek R1 671B.
Engage securely via private connectivity — possible through AWS PrivateLink for low-latency, secure integration between AWS workloads and SambaNova Cloud.
“With the SambaNova platform running in AWS Marketplace, organizations gain access to secure, high-speed inference from the largest open-source models. Solutions like this will help businesses move from experimentation to full production with AI,” said Michele Rosen, Research Manager, Open GenAI, LLMs, and the Evolving Open Source, IDC.
This tight integration enables customers to deploy high-performance, multi-tenant inference solutions without the need to purchase or manage custom hardware — expanding SambaNova’s reach into enterprise environments where time-to-value and IT friction have historically limited adoption.
Making High-Performance Inference More Accessible
With this listing in AWS Marketplace, SambaNova is meeting enterprise customers where they already are — within their trusted cloud environments and procurement frameworks. By removing onboarding friction and offering seamless integration, SambaNova makes it easier than ever for organizations to evaluate, deploy, and scale high-performance inference solutions.
“This makes it dramatically easier for customers to start using SambaNova — no new contracts, no long onboarding — just click and go,” said Liang.
Availability
SambaNova’s inference platform is available immediately in AWS Marketplace. Enterprise customers can visit the SambaNova listing in AWS Marketplace to get started.
About SambaNova
Customers turn to SambaNova to quickly deploy state-of-the-art generative AI capabilities within the enterprise. Our purpose-built enterprise-scale AI platform is the technology backbone for the next generation of AI computing.
Headquartered in Palo Alto, California, SambaNova Systems was founded in 2017 by industry luminaries, and hardware and software design experts from Sun/Oracle and Stanford University. Investors include SoftBank Vision Fund 2, funds and accounts managed by BlackRock, Intel Capital, GV, Walden International, Temasek, GIC, Redline Capital, Atlantic Bridge Ventures, Celesta, and several others. Visit us at sambanova.ai or contact us at info@sambanova.ai . Follow SambaNova Systems on LinkedIn and on X . To learn more, visit www.sambanova.ai

title_original: Hugging Face introduces Inference Providers for unified AI access
body_original:
🤗 Exciting update from Hugging Face: Introducing Inference Providers!
🤗 Exciting update from Hugging Face: Introducing Inference Providers!
This game-changing feature unifies 15+ top inference partners—including Novita AI, Cerebras Systems, Nebius AI, Featherless AI, Together AI, Fireworks AI, Groq, Hyperbolic, Cohere, fal, Nscale, SambaNova, and Replicate under a single, OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
No more juggling multiple APIs or wrestling with infrastructure headaches. Prototype your AI ideas today and scale seamlessly to production tomorrow, all with the same unified API. It's developer-friendly magic that lets you focus on innovation, not ops. Check out the Inference Providers / Models Support Matrix to see which models are supported by each provider at https://lnkd.in/gxaimbQF
Here are a few sample use cases:
Customer Service Automation: Embed advanced LLMs (e.g., via Cohere or Groq) for hyper-personalized chatbots that handle queries in real-time, improving response times without provisioning your own GPU clusters.
Predictive Sales Analytics: Leverage models from providers like Together AI forecast leads and opportunities more accurately, transitioning from dev prototypes to enterprise-scale deployments effortlessly.
Marketing Content Generation: Use image or text generation models (e.g., from fal or Replicate) directly in marketing applications to create dynamic visuals or copy for campaigns, all through one API, no infra setup required.
This lowers barriers for AI adoption in CRM workflows, making it easier to deliver value fast. For instance, explore affordable GPU options from providers like Novita AI: https://novita.ai
Question for the community: Which type of user or developer do you think will benefit most from this capability—data scientists prototyping models, full-stack devs building apps, or startups scaling on a budget? Drop your thoughts below! 👇
Get started here: https://lnkd.in/gu6XeCZw

title_original: Anthropic Claude Marketplace: Enterprise AI Buying Simplified | Daily AI Briefing | David & Goliath
body_original:
Skip to main content
Daily AI Briefing / Enterprise AI
Anthropic Launches a Marketplace to Simplify Enterprise AI Buying
Sunday 15 March 2026 | Anthropic | AI Growth Engine Employee Amplification Systems Secure AI Brain
Anthropic launched the Claude Marketplace on 6 March 2026, allowing enterprise customers to apply existing Claude API spending commitments toward third-party applications built on Claude. Launch partners include Snowflake, GitLab, Harvey, Replit, and Lovable Labs. Anthropic is taking no commission at launch, positioning itself as an enterprise procurement layer rather than just a model provider.
Operator Insight
Anthropic is not just building a marketplace. It is building a procurement consolidation strategy. By making it easy to buy AI software through a single invoice and forgoing commission now, Anthropic is making it progressively more expensive to leave the Claude ecosystem later. Operators who benefit from the convenience today need to weigh that against the switching costs they are accumulating.
30-Second Summary
Anthropic launched the Claude Marketplace on 6 March 2026, creating a curated storefront where enterprise customers can apply existing Claude API spending commitments toward third-party applications built on Claude. The model mirrors AWS and Azure cloud marketplaces but with one key difference: Anthropic is forgoing commission at launch to accelerate ecosystem growth. For operators, the marketplace makes AI procurement simpler. It also, quietly, makes leaving the Claude ecosystem harder.
At a Glance
Topic: Enterprise AI
Company: Anthropic
Date: 6 March 2026
Announcement: Anthropic launches a marketplace allowing enterprise customers to apply Claude spending commitments toward third-party Claude-powered applications
What Changed: Enterprise AI procurement can now be consolidated through a single Anthropic invoice, covering both model access and partner software subscriptions
Why It Matters: Anthropic is positioning itself as a distribution and procurement layer for enterprise AI, not just a model and API provider
Who Should Care: CIOs, CFOs, procurement leads, and operators currently managing multiple AI software contracts
Key Facts
Company: Anthropic
Launch Date: 6 March 2026 (limited preview)
What Changed: Enterprise customers can apply unused Claude API commitments toward third-party app subscriptions within the marketplace
Who It Affects: Organisations with existing Anthropic enterprise contracts and procurement teams managing AI software spend
Primary Source: Anthropic announcement, VentureBeat, SiliconAngle, The Next Web
What Happened
Anthropic launched the Claude Marketplace in limited preview on 6 March 2026, at a moment when enterprise AI spending is accelerating and procurement teams are struggling to manage a growing stack of specialised AI tools.
The core mechanic is straightforward: organisations that have committed annual API spend with Anthropic can redirect a portion of that budget toward software applications built on Claude by third-party developers. Rather than issuing separate purchase orders for each tool, finance teams receive a single consolidated invoice from Anthropic. No commission is taken on those transactions at launch.
Six launch partners are available at preview: Snowflake (data infrastructure), GitLab (software development), Harvey (legal AI), Rogo (financial analysis), Replit (coding), and Lovable Labs (no-code application building). Each partner's application runs on Claude models, meaning AI quality and safety standards remain consistent across the marketplace.
Why It Matters
Consolidating AI software procurement through a single vendor reduces administrative overhead for enterprise procurement teams
The no-commission model at launch makes the economics attractive for both partners and customers in the short term
Specialist tools for legal (Harvey), finance (Rogo), and code (GitLab) address high-value operator workflows with pre-built, Claude-native applications
The model mirrors the AWS and Azure marketplace strategy, which has proven highly effective at deepening customer relationships and increasing switching costs over time
Anthropic shifts from pure model provider to platform and distribution layer, a significant change in competitive positioning
The David and Goliath View
The Claude Marketplace is being presented as a procurement convenience. It is also a consolidation strategy. By making it easier to buy AI software through a single Anthropic invoice, the company is creating a gravitational pull that makes it progressively more expensive to work with other model providers. AWS and Azure built the same moat. The cloud marketplace model works.
For operators of lean organisations, the appeal is genuine. Instead of evaluating, contracting, and paying for Harvey, Replit, and Snowflake separately, you use existing Claude budget to access all three and manage one invoice. The friction reduction is real, and the quality guarantee of Claude-native tools matters when you cannot afford to test everything yourself.
The sharper question is what happens when Anthropic introduces commission structures, or when a non-Claude tool does the job better and your procurement is already locked in. Operators benefit most from this marketplace if they treat it as a discovery and evaluation layer, not a permanent procurement strategy.
Start with one partner tool. Validate the outcome. Keep your vendor diversification options open.
Where This Fits in the AI Stack
AI Growth Engine: Rogo (financial analysis) and Harvey (legal) are directly applicable to revenue-critical workflows. Access through existing Claude commitments lowers the barrier to piloting high-value AI tools in deal analysis and contract review.
Employee Amplification Systems: GitLab and Replit enable development teams to accelerate code production using Claude-native tools. Lovable Labs opens no-code application building to non-technical staff, expanding the automation surface across the organisation.
Secure AI Brain: Claude Marketplace applications inherit Anthropic's enterprise security standards, including data handling policies and SOC 2 compliance. Consolidating AI tools within a single audited ecosystem simplifies the security review process for risk and compliance teams.
Questions Operators Are Asking
Should we move all our AI software procurement to the Claude Marketplace?
Not necessarily. The marketplace is a useful discovery and procurement channel, but consolidating all AI spend with a single provider creates dependency. Use it for tools where Claude's capabilities are clearly the right fit. Maintain optionality elsewhere.
Is the no-commission model permanent?
No. Anthropic has stated it is forgoing commission at launch to accelerate ecosystem growth. Pricing structures will likely evolve once the marketplace achieves sufficient scale. Factor this into any multi-year budget planning.
Which launch partners are most relevant to smaller organisations?
Harvey (legal), Replit (coding), and Rogo (financial analysis) are the three most immediately useful for operators running lean teams. Each addresses a high-cost, high-expertise function that is typically bottlenecked at smaller organisations.
How is this different from AWS or Azure marketplaces?
The mechanics are similar: redirect existing cloud commitments toward partner software. The difference is that Anthropic's marketplace is model-centric rather than infrastructure-centric, meaning every tool in the catalogue is built on Claude and inherits consistent AI behaviour and safety standards.
Citable Summary
What happened: Anthropic launched the Claude Marketplace in limited preview on 6 March 2026, allowing enterprise customers to apply existing Claude API spending commitments toward third-party Claude-powered applications. Launch partners include Snowflake, GitLab, Harvey, Rogo, Replit, and Lovable Labs. No commission is taken at launch.
Why it matters: Anthropic is repositioning from model provider to enterprise platform and procurement layer, mirroring the marketplace strategies of AWS and Azure. The model reduces procurement friction for operators while deepening dependency on the Claude ecosystem.
David and Goliath view: The convenience is real, but so is the consolidation play. Use the Claude Marketplace as a discovery and evaluation layer. Validate tools on merit, keep vendor options open, and plan for commission structures that will likely follow once Anthropic achieves market concentration.
Offer relevance:
AI Growth Engine: Harvey and Rogo provide Claude-native tools for legal and financial analysis in revenue workflows
Employee Amplification Systems: GitLab, Replit, and Lovable Labs extend AI-assisted work across development and no-code teams
Secure AI Brain: marketplace applications inherit Anthropic's enterprise security standards, simplifying compliance review
Why This Matters for Operators
✓ The Claude Marketplace makes AI procurement faster and simpler today, but map your dependency on the Claude ecosystem before consolidating budgets there.
✓ Harvey (legal), GitLab (code), and Snowflake (data) are the three launch partners most relevant to operator workflows. Evaluate them on merit, not just because they appear in the same catalogue.
✓ No commission at launch is a deliberate growth play. Expect commission structures to appear once Anthropic achieves sufficient market concentration.
✓ More specialist applications will appear in the marketplace catalogue over the coming months.

title_original: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B - Hugging Face
body_original:
Model Information
The Meta Llama 3.1 collection of multilingual large language models (LLMs) is a collection of pretrained and instruction tuned generative models in 8B, 70B and 405B sizes (text in/text out). The Llama 3.1 instruction tuned text only models (8B, 70B, 405B) are optimized for multilingual dialogue use cases and outperform many of the available open source and closed chat models on common industry benchmarks.
Model developer : Meta
Model Architecture: Llama 3.1 is an auto-regressive language model that uses an optimized transformer architecture. The tuned versions use supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning with human feedback (RLHF) to align with human preferences for helpfulness and safety.
Training Data
Params
Input modalities
Output modalities

GQA

Knowledge cutoff
Llama 3.1 (text only)
A new mix of publicly available online data.
